title: Contingency Instruction Cache Plan Bundle
description: A conceptual CIC plan bundle with a short main plan, two to four cached contingencies, and replan_if conditions.

task.description: The task for which this plan bundle was prepared.

world_state_summary.description: An external summary consumed by CIC; CIC does not build or validate the world state.

main_plan.description: A short sequence for the expected path.

cached_contingencies.description: Two to four short-lived cached contingencies considered during execution.

replan_if.description: Conditions indicating that the plan bundle is invalid, the event is unknown, or the situation is high-risk.

contingency.description: A cached contingency with a trigger, bounded instruction, validity condition, expiration time, and fallback.

event.description: A stable identifier for the event represented by this cached contingency.

priority.description: A relative arbitration hint, not an event probability or safety level.

trigger.description: A condition that can be matched against an observation during execution.

instruction.description: The cached instruction considered when trigger matches and valid_if holds.

valid_if.description: A condition that must still hold before the cached instruction can be used.

expire_after_ms.description: The maximum age of this cached contingency after the plan bundle is loaded.

fallback.description: A bounded fallback instruction used when this cached contingency cannot be applied; high-risk cases should be routed to an external fallback or replanning path rather than handled as ordinary cache hits.
